title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek public's help to identify a second juvenile in a West Ninth Street lemonade stand robbery.
Surveillance photos of the unidentified suspect show him in black pants, a black hat, and carrying a black backpack.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested for the June 10 robbery; the siblings reopened the stand to community support.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is exploring options for his final NBA team, leaving the Lakers after eight seasons.
Rich Paul, James's business partner, mentioned the Celtics, with emphasis on Brad Stevens and Jayson Tatum, as a potential landing spot.
The Celtics are not among the top four contenders for James, which include Philadelphia, Miami, Denver, and Minnesota.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticized the Boston Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish compared the Brown trade to the negative impact of the Dallas Mavericks trading Luka Doncic.
Parish believes Jaylen Brown likely deserves a jersey retirement for his contributions to the Celtics.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum shared a farewell message to Jaylen Brown after Brown's trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown was traded for Paul George, two first-round draft picks, and two second-round picks.
Brown felt disrespected by Celtics management and did not explicitly say goodbye to Tatum.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics extend Neemias Queta's contract by four years, securing him through 2030-31 for $56 million.
Queta's previous team option for 2026-27 was picked up, with the new deal totaling five years of commitment.
The center averaged 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks in the 2025-26 season, starting 75 games for Boston.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates its 200th anniversary as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
Established in 1826, the restaurant's building dates back to around 1716 and is a National Historic Landmark.
Executive Chef Americo DiFronzo oversees a menu that maintains 40% of its original offerings, including clam chowder.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's summer events include the Boston Pops Fireworks on July 4, featuring a performance by Jane Lynch.
Gillette Stadium will host the last World Cup quarterfinal match in Boston on July 9, drawing significant crowds.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail, featuring 60 tall ships, will commence in Boston Harbor on July 11.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston, the oldest operating restaurant in the U.S., has had numerous celebrity guests.
Six American presidents, including Barack Obama and John F. Kennedy, have dined at the Union Oyster House.
Celebrities from Matt Damon to Meryl Streep have visited, with artist Stan Kotzen capturing many in a 2003 caricature.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross of Greenland, NH, a 19-year State Police veteran, died unexpectedly at 41.
Ross served in several roles, recently leading the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit.
A fund is established for Ross's family; services are scheduled for July 6-7 in Hampton, NH.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized the Boston Celtics' communication after his trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown's trade to the Sixers involved Paul George and four draft picks; he was puzzled by the lack of explanation.
Celtics' Brad Stevens is criticized for handling trade talks; Brown expressed feeling disrespected in the process.
